messages.message: I intend to move my army from VEN to TRI to assert a stronger presence in the Adriatic. If you don't mind, I'd prefer to avoid conflict. Let me know your plans.
I'm considering securing southern Italy more firmly. If you're focused on the west, we might have room to grow without stepping on each other's toes. What are your intentions?
I don't plan to challenge your eastern provinces directly, but I may need to move into APU or ION for expansion. Let me know if that's a concern.
I plan to move my army from VEN to TRI to strengthen my position in the region. I hope this doesn't interfere with your plans. Let me know if you foresee any issues.

Hi Italy,
I have no problems with that. Would you like to share intel with one another as the game goes on? I reckon we could help one another out quite a bit

Hey France, hope you are well! Just reaching out to establish communication between us. We have a couple of things to discuss:
1) What to do about Burgundy?
2) What to do about Belgium?
I'd be down to DMZ Burgundy if you'd be down to do the same, but if you have something you'd like to do with your unit in Paris I understand. Either way I hope we can have good communication and work together!

Hi France!

Good luck in this game, I hope we can be allies and help each other as the game goes on.

Regards, Turkey

Hey Turkey! Good luck to you too, and I'm sure we can share info and help each other out.

Regards,
France
I intend to secure southern Italy further by possibly moving into APU or ION. If your focus is on the west, I believe we can expand without conflict. What are your thoughts?

Hello France! I was wondering what your thoughts are on the current situation. Obviously we'd both do better if we can stay out of each other's hair. If you'd like to help me into Belgium in the fall, I'd be happy to support you to Burgundy and work with you against Germany.

Hey Italy! Good luck for the game.
I think IR is a really strong alliance, it has a lot of potential to come to Italy's aid if Austria gets a bit agressive or Turkey get's far ahead in the Med. We'd be able to help each other out from the start and we don't get in each other's way (unless you plan on attacking Austria, but that's pretty unconventional).
What do you think about it?
I may need to move into ION or APU for expansion. I don't wish to challenge your territory directly, but I’d appreciate knowing if this might concern you.

I would very much prefer not to see an army in Venice this turn, especially since that army will be stronger moving south than moving north, or west. Let me know if there's any way I could talk you out of that plan.

Hello Russia! I would be interested in working with you if possible. I will be moving my fleets to Norwegian and North Seas and plan to move to Norway in the fall. However, if you'd like to work with me against Germany, I can support you to Denmark in the fall and then focus on France.

What are your thoughts?

Hi Russia,
Good luck for the game. I hope that we can be allies. Shall we start by bouncing in the black sea?

Thank you for your support, Turkey! I appreciate the offer to share intel — I think cooperation between our powers could be mutually beneficial. I'll keep your interests in mind as I expand southward.
Thank you, I'd appreciate if you kept me in the loop regarding your plans as well.

Hey Turkey! Good luck to you too, and I'm sure we can share info and help each other out.

Regards,
France

Hello Turkey. Good luck for the game too.
I definitely agree with you: a bounce in the Black Sea is a good start. How do you think we should deal with Austria and the Balkans in the first few years?

Russia, I appreciate your kind words and the idea of an IR alliance. I'm focused on securing southern Italy and expanding cautiously. I don't plan to challenge Austria directly yet, but I'm open to supporting each other if tensions rise.
Hello Turkey! I am curious as to what your relationships with Russia and Austria are.
Sounds good to me

Hello England! I think we both would benefit from working together in this game. I would much rather work with you against Germany than the other way around, and I think helping you into Belgium would be the best move.

I would like to DMZ the English Channel, and I think it would be best if we can agree to split Belgium/holland between the two of us. I would like to support you into Belgium.

Let me know your thoughts.

Austria, I understand your concerns about Venice. I intend to move my army from VEN to TRI to strengthen my Adriatic position. I have no interest in provoking conflict — if there's a compromise or alternative route, I'm open to hearing it.

Hey Germany. Good luck for the game.
Have you heard from England yet? He was trying to get me to go against you, but I'm not really interested in getting so involved in the West so early.
Hello Austria! I would be interested in what your current relationship with Russia is. I plan to open by moving to Norwegian and North Seas.

Hello England.
I am pretty friendly with both at the moment, why do you ask?
France, I'm still considering my southern moves — perhaps into APU or ION. If you're focused westward, I believe we can expand safely without interference. What are your plans?
Hello Italy! I was just wondering what your relationships are with France.
That sounds good. So you'll focus on the Mediterranean and not attack Austria or Turkey directly yet? How will you deal with France then?
That is a good idea. I will move to North Sea and Norwegian Seas this turn.

Hey Germany, I like the idea of a DMZ in Burgundy, but I also like to plan for a bounce. Would that be fine?

As for Belgium, if you support me in to Belgium, I will support you into North Sea in the following year, which will likely put us in a strong position to defeat England. What do you think?

Hello England. Good luck for the game.
Yes, I'd like to work with you and get Scandinavia sorted out early. I don't like it when Germany gets three builds in 1901 and neither do you, so I'm all for it.
I have neutral relations with France currently — we’re not at odds, but I’m focused on securing southern Italy and the Mediterranean. I don’t foresee any conflict with their westward interests.

Hi Austria,
How do you think we should play the Balkans?

Hey England, hope you are well! I just wanted to open up communication between us and hopefully be able to work together later on!
First, I'd like to discuss the topic of Belgium. There are several options regarding this, and I'm open to suggestions from you.

We also have the option of blocking Russia out of Scandinavia and the North. A convoy into Norway coupled with a bounce in Sweden will stop a Russian build. Let me know your thoughts!

Hello France. Good luck for the game.
Have you heard from England yet? He was trying to get me to attack Germany, but I'm not really interested in getting involved in the West so early.
Alright, well, keep me updated. If there's anything I can do to help you out, just let me know.

Correct — I plan to focus on Mediterranean expansion and will avoid direct confrontation with Austria or Turkey for now. Regarding France, I aim to keep the south secure while avoiding interference with their goals. We can support each other if tensions rise.
Sounds good to me!
Hello Germany! I am not planning on going to Belgium this turn, so you can definitely have it. I will move to Norwegian and North Seas in the spring.
Have you heard anything from Germany?
I'm happy to let you take Rumania and then push Austria back. I'll try to get Serbia and then push north
Hey Italy, I'm still weighing my options, but I'm trying to avoid an early war with you, as that often doesn't end well for either of us.
Hey Russia, thanks for the information. England hasn't told me much yet, so I'll let you know what he has to say!

Hello Russia, good luck to you as well. I haven't heard anything from Germany, but England has been talking to me about going after Germany, so you might have a good opening there.

In any case, I'll let you know if I hear anything that might be of interest to you.
You?

I will take Serbia for sure, and would like Greece as well. I think Russia will probably go to Rumania in the spring and then support himself into the black sea in the fall. We could try to bounce him.
Alright, sounds good. Thanks for the response!
Absolutely, I’ll keep you updated on my plans as we both look to expand. If I move into ION or APU, I’ll ensure it doesn’t threaten your positions.

Hey Austria, hope you are well! I just wanted to open up communication between us and hopefully be able to work together later on!
First, I'd like to discuss the topic of Russia. There are several options regarding this, and I'm open to suggestions from you.

First off, the option of an Anschluss. If we can have good communication and work together I think we can both do really well in this game.
Not much. Just that they plan to go to Holland in the spring.
I think we can do that

Hey Turkey, hope you are well! I just wanted to open up communication between us and hopefully be able to work together later on!
First, I'd like to discuss the topic of Russia. There are several options regarding this, and I'm open to suggestions from you.

Hi Germany,

Good to hear from you!

We could be good allies at some point. How are your relations with your neighbours?
Great! Likewise I'll let you know what I find out.
I have no intention of antagonizing Russia right now, but we can speak again after the spring moves and see how we can work together.
Great. Let's keep each other posted.
I'm currently trying to ally with both Austria and Russia.
Alright, sounds like a plan.
Great, I'll keep you posted too.
Yes, that sounds good.
I’m still considering my next moves — likely securing APU or ION to strengthen southern Italy. If your focus remains westward, we should be able to expand without stepping on each other’s toes.
Have you spoken to Italy?
Hi Russia, what are your plans for Galicia? I would rather not bounce there.
Alright. Let's keep each other posted.
I have not spoken with Russia yet, but he seems to have no plans to head my way.
How are your relations with Austria?
Hi France, we won't be encountering each other for a while, but let me know if there's any way we can work together.
Relations with my neighbors are solid so far, no quarrels or anything.
I really don't want to waste a unit bouncing at Trieste. Turkey and I are discussing Russia, so if you'd like to join in, you'd have a great chance to grab some ground in the Med.

Hey Austria. Good luck for the game.
I think a bounce in Galicia is fine. We don't need to show our cards so early, it can even be good if other players think that we're fighting.
That sounds good to me. I'm assuming you and Russia have Dmz'd Galicia?

Not very good yet, he wants a bounce in Galicia and I'm guessing he wants Italy to stay off his back, and that's a no-go for me.
How are your talks with Austria and Italy? I think Italy will go after France, at least that's what he told me.

I appreciate your concerns about Trieste. I have no interest in creating friction — moving into TRI is purely to strengthen my position in the Adriatic. If Russia and Turkey are coordinating, I’d be happy to stay informed and avoid stepping on your toes.
A little. He says he will not move into Trieste, but that I don't believe him.
I haven't heard much from Italy or France but I'll see what I can find out
Alright, that's good to know.
We're bouncing there.
I’m moving forward with securing southern Italy, possibly into ION or APU. I’ll make sure these moves don’t threaten your holdings. Let me know if you foresee any issues with my plans.

Hey Italy, hope you are well! I just wanted to open up communication between us and hopefully be able to work together later on!

It's important for the both of us to have a good relationship and exchange information. Austria and Russia have Dmz'd Galicia, so I'd assume the Russian army in Warsaw will move towards me. I'd also assume that Austria will want to work with Turkey to start off.

Have you spoken with France at all? He sent me one message.
Me neither really.
He has not said anything about heading your way yet.
Yeah I wouldn't believe him either.
Alright, let's do it.
Alright, I'll let you know too
Will do
Good to know. Thanks.
He said he's moving into ION
Thanks again for your support. I’m focusing on securing the Med and will keep you updated if there’s an opportunity for mutual benefit or coordination.
Hey Austria, good luck to you in the game. And yes, I'll let you know if I hear any information that might be of use to you.
That's quite weird to me. How have your relations with Russia been so far?
Thanks. Same to you.
I hope you understand that I am leaning towards an opening where I move to Pie, but this is not intended as aggressive towards you
That would be great for me, but might leave you open to take Greece.
I’m still weighing my southern options — likely moving into APU or ION to strengthen Italy’s hold. If you’re moving westward, we should have room to grow without conflict.
So if we bounce Burgundy, what are you planning to do with Paris? Have you spoken with Italy? I'm interested in knowing how Italy plans to move.

Of course.
I think Austria is pretty friendly with you. He asked me for a bounce in Galicia.

I appreciate your clarification about moving to PIE — that sounds like a safe westward move. I’ll proceed with securing APU or ION to strengthen southern Italy, and I’ll keep your position in mind to avoid any overlap.
I've spoken with him a little. He seems fairly reasonable to me.
I'll give it back if you take Rumania
Seems like he's not a talker.
I see Russia mentioned an action in Galicia — if you're coordinating, I'll keep an eye on the region. I remain committed to moving VEN to TRI to strengthen my Adriatic presence without conflict.
Yeah it seems so. Oh well
Has Russia said anything about me?
Alright. I'll make sure you don't waste a turn by bouncing in Venice.
Italy just told me he's going for France
He said he's heading to Galicia. That's all he's said to me.
I'm told Italy is going for France
Have you heard from Italy yet?
Sounds good
Alright, thanks for the info.
Great. That might make Austria even more vulnerable
Okay, thanks for letting me know.
He's said he's moving west.
Okay, thanks for the info.
Understood — I’ll continue to ensure my moves in ION or APU don’t threaten your positions. Let me know if you foresee any issues with my plans.
Turkey says you're heading west. What's your plan?
Oh really? Hmm I might watch out then
Hmm I doubt I'll be able to help you if you're attacking France. Good luck though
I will probably hold Paris, and I'm trying to decide between holding Marseilles or moving it to Spain
Any word from Russia?
Italy said he's moving West?
Do you think I should block him out of Greece or let him take it?
I have no plans to challenge you directly. My move from VEN to TRI is purely defensive and strategic, not aggressive. If you're focused eastward or northward, I believe we can coexist peacefully.
I'm being told by another player that Russia plans to head North. Do you know anything about this?
He might just be going for Tunis
If you were heading west, I would appreciate an army in Tuscany instead.

I have no intention of attacking France or challenging your interests. My focus is securing southern Italy through APU or ION — positions that do not threaten your holdings. I remain open to cooperation.
I think we're bouncing in Galicia.
I appreciate your confirmation of moving to PIE — that leaves APU and ION open for me to strengthen southern Italy. If you're moving westward, I believe we can expand safely without conflict.
I also think we have a good opportunity to work together in the early game, which I hope we can continue to do.
Turkey told me Italy is moving west. Not sure if that's true, but just a heads up.
They did not mention that to me, so I cannot say for sure.

Thank you for your feedback. I can adjust my plans — instead of moving to TRI, I will consider moving my army from VEN to TUS to support southern Italy indirectly while avoiding any potential friction. I hope this aligns better with your strategic goals.
I think he should be ok with getting Tunis this year. A bounce in Greece is definitely better than nothing, unless Italy starts putting pressure on Austria and goes for Trieste.

I appreciate your thoughts on cooperation. I plan to move into APU or ION to strengthen southern Italy’s position. If you continue westward into PIE or beyond, we should have plenty of room to grow without stepping on each other’s toes. Let me know if you foresee any issues!

I understand your concerns — I do not plan to attack France directly. My goal is to strengthen southern Italy by moving into APU or ION, positions that do not threaten your interests. I remain open to cooperation if the need arises.
Hm. I wonder who that third party is.
I appreciate the heads up. Italy has told me they are moving west, but I don't have a firm confirmation of that.
But a bounce would look way more suspicious

I’ve decided to move my army from VEN to TUS instead of TRI to avoid any potential friction. This will support southern Italy’s security without stepping on your plans. If you're moving westward into PIE or beyond, I believe we can grow safely.
I don't know. Turkey seemed rather interested in what I told them, so I don't know if you can trust them.
That would be great. Thanks.
Sounds good to me
I can't see Turkey lying to me like that. What is he up to?

I’ve adjusted my plans and will move A VEN to TUS rather than TRI to avoid any conflict. This move supports southern Italy’s stability and aligns better with your strategic focus. I hope this is agreeable.
Okay, thanks for letting me know. I'll keep you updated with anything that comes my way.

I appreciate your openness to cooperation. I intend to move A VEN to TUS to strengthen southern Italy’s position without interfering with your westward goals. If you're moving into PIE or beyond, I believe we’ll have room to grow together.
You as well
Great, thanks.
Yeah that's fair. And we'll see how the Spring moves shake out
I have no idea. I'd say they might be lying to me, but that's up to them.
Agreed. Thanks.

ITALY: A ROM S A VEN - TUS

messages.message: You still down for that?
Yeah, I'm still going to bounce in Burgundy
I'm supporting North Sea to Belgium
I thought you said you didn't want a bounce?
Thanks for keeping your word. It looks like we've got a couple of inexperienced players, so we should have no problem carving up Turkey and Russia.
And I'll bounce Germany in Burgundy
Nice that our opening looks friendly to Austria. That army in Apulia is very nice.
We did. I thought that was what we agreed on. If you want Rumania, I'll support you there from Ukraine.
Okay, cool!
How are you feeling about the Spring moves?
Tough opening. Why the move to Gascony?
Hey Russia, what are your thoughts on Sweden?
That worked perfectly.
Indeed! What's your plan for next turn?
It's just more flexible in terms of who I can work with, although moving to Brest would've been even better
I agree, it was really good for us!
Thank you for your support. I've adjusted my plans and will move A VEN to TUS rather than TRI to avoid conflict and ensure regional stability. I hope this aligns with your strategic direction.
Great, let's keep this up.
Yep, sounds good to me.
Yes, I thought it was, sorry.
Any reason why you think I should move to Picardy instead of Burgundy?
I think I'm going to try and block Austria from taking Greece
Seems he changed his mind
Excellent.
Alright, sounds good.
What are your thoughts?
Also, I'm moving to Brest to disguise that I'm working with you
Thanks! This is what we agreed on: I take Serbia, you take Greece, and then I support you into Rumania, right?
Russia thinks I'm going to support him into Rumania, so you should get in unopposed.
It happens.
So are you working with Germany, then?
So are you still planning to move to Marseilles?
Yeah, it does seem like that
No, Picardy is fine

Yes, that sounds good.
Are we still going to bounce in Black Sea?

It's okay, it happens.
So I think you're going to support yourself into Greece?
Okay, thank you
What are you thinking of doing with Spain and Marseilles this turn?
I remain focused on securing southern Italy through APU or ION and will ensure these moves do not threaten your positions. I remain open to cooperation if needed.
Sure, let's do that
For the time being
What's your plan regarding Russia? Are you bouncing him in Sweden?
Great
I'm holding Marseilles, but I'm not sure what I'm going to do with Spain yet
And what about you?

I appreciate your confidence in our coordination, and I agree we could work together effectively against Turkey or Russia in the future. For now, I remain focused on securing southern Italy and will continue to avoid any moves that threaten your interests.
Thanks for letting me know.
I haven't decided. What's your plan regarding Russia?
Seems like Austria changed his mind about attacking France. How are your relations with him?

Trying to maintain good relations with Austria, and we're bouncing in the Black Sea.
Do you have an alliance with Austria?
I have no intention of moving toward Marseilles or interfering with your western operations. My focus is firmly on securing Naples and strengthening my position in the south.
How are your relations with Italy?
Currently on good terms, although I'm still weighing my options
How are your relations with Italy?

Hello Germany! I will be moving to Norway with my army. I will be leaving Belgium open for you to take.

I am worried that we may have a Juggernaut, so I think it might be wise to move on Russia soon. What do you think?
Sounds good to me
Russia and I have agreed to bounce in the Black Sea again, so I won't be able to take it.
Ok, good to know. I will be moving my army to Belgium.
Why would Russia agree to that?
That move is highly unusual.
Hey England, I would be down to move on Russia with you.
Are you going to keep Russia out of Sweden?
And what about Venice?
But I'm just saying that if you'd rather do something else with your army in Norway, that's fine too
Indeed
Great
I noticed your overture earlier—I’m open to discussing Mediterranean strategies further. If you’re interested in cooperation or coordination against Turkey, I’d be happy to explore it.
Yes, and in the long term, I'd like to split up Turkey and the Balkans between the two of us.
I'm not sure yet
I could use your help. Russia and Turkey are apparently coordinating.
How are your relations with Germany and England?
I can help you. I'm bouncing him out.
How are your relations with Germany and Russia?
I think he wants to protect Rumania
How are your relations with England and Russia?
Good with both. Germany seems willing to focus on Russia and I think I'm on good terms with Russia too.
Excellent. Thank you.
But wouldn't moving into Galicia have been a better option if he wanted to protect Rumania?
Yeah, that's the plan right now. Are you going to be moving against Russia or Austria?

Austria's focus has shifted toward the Balkans, but I remain neutral in those affairs. My relations with him are stable and cooperative—my goal is southern consolidation, not eastern conflict. If Turkey's ambitions shift toward Naples or ION, I’d appreciate knowing ahead of time.
I will be convoying my army to Norway this turn. I wanted to let you know so that you don't think I am trying to put you in an awkward position.
Cool
Nice opening for you, you're guaranteed to take Belgium.
I asked Turkey if he was planning on moving on you or Russia, but he's not really responsive. I'll let you know if I get any information
True. He doesn't really have any units he can commit there though.
England seems more trustworthy than Russia, but we'll have to see. How about yours with them?
Alright, thanks for letting me know.
Alright, thanks.
That's fine. I was thinking about taking Sweden this turn, would you mind if I do that?
Yeah, although I am worried that England and Germany are working together.
If you could convince England to work with me against Germany, it would be much appreciated
I haven't decided yet.
Will you be convoying to Norway?
Right.
What do you think?
That sounds good. For now, it'd probably be a good idea to not put too much pressure on Turkey yet. That way we could both take a few Scs from the Balkans before turning towards him.
Fairly good with both, although I'm much more worried about England than Russia
Sure thing

Venice remains unthreatened by my current plans—I have no interest in moving toward it or provoking Austria. My focus is firmly on Naples and strengthening southern Italy. I hope our mutual neutrality remains intact.
Great. I think I'll wait another year or two before deciding who to target first, but I like the idea of us working together in the Med and the Balkans.
Agreed.
I’m still interested in hearing more about your Mediterranean strategy—I suspect your interest in southern expansion may align with mine. If you’re open to coordinating future moves, let me know.
I can try that. I'll try to get Germany and England to focus on each other instead of you.

I appreciate your patience and openness to future coordination. While I focus on securing Naples and strengthening southern Italy this Fall, I remain open to aligning our strategies as your Mediterranean ambitions crystallize. Let me know if you'd like to explore joint plans against Turkey or other powers.
Yeah, you have any reason for that?
Still trying to decide

I appreciate your confirmation. My movements remain focused on securing Naples and maintaining strength in southern Italy—I have no intention of encroaching on your eastern interests. I remain open to cooperation if your goals align.
So do you want to bounce again in Burgundy or will you be moving Paris to Picardy?
As do I
Thank you
I would like to bounce again
Fair enough. Just keep me updated so I can help you out
I'm not moving into ION
Yes, that is okay with me.
Although it might make more sense for me to hold this turn, as England is likely to go for Belgium
I'll stay out of it
What are you doing with Paris then?

I intend to move Fleet ION to Naples this Fall to secure the supply center, with Army ROM supporting the move. I will continue to avoid any actions threatening your interests in TRIESTE or VENICE. I hope this aligns with your strategic direction.
As do I — I appreciate your neutrality and will continue to focus on southern expansion without encroaching on your western interests. Let’s keep it that way.
Will do.
Good luck!
I’m glad to hear you’ll stay out of ION. That allows me to consolidate Naples securely without eastern complications. I look forward to maintaining our mutual understanding.
Yes, I'd like to. For now I don't have any plans with my southern units, but if Austria and Turkey become a threat I'd like to help you out against them.
I remain open to discussing Mediterranean coordination in greater detail soon. For now, I’m moving Fleet ION to Naples with Army ROM’s support to strengthen southern Italy.
Thanks
I'd prefer not to. At least not this turn.
Alright
I look forward to a fruitful relationship.
Holding
Okay, then I won't bounce Sweden.
I'm going to be letting you into Sweden.
Good luck against France

Thank you for your response. I appreciate your openness—I’m confident our paths may align in the future. For now, I’m focused on securing Naples this Fall, with Army ROM supporting Fleet ION’s move. If you’re considering southern expansion or have plans for the Black Sea, I’d welcome further discussion.
Alright, cool.
Alright. What are your plans with England and France?

Thank you for your kind wishes and support. I’m glad we’ve established mutual understanding—I’ll continue to ensure my movements remain focused on Naples and southern Italy, allowing both of us to operate without conflict. If your plans evolve toward Mediterranean expansion, I’d appreciate early insight.
Yes, I'm also not committing to anything just yet. I'm also going to take Rumania.
No solid plans yet. Both of them want to work with me and haven't given me a reason not to.

I want to reaffirm my commitment to peaceful relations. My Fall order reinforces southern Italy’s stability by securing Naples with Fleet ION and Army ROM’s support. I remain focused on growth without encroaching on your interests—I hope this aligns with your strategic direction.
It seems that France wants to work with you, and England wants to work with me and it sounds like he wants to go after France.
So I'll probably just stay out of it for now, since there's no real need to get involved.

I remain committed to non-interference in your western operations. My focus is securing Naples and strengthening southern Italy—I will continue to avoid any moves toward Marseilles or PIE. Let’s keep this mutual understanding intact.
Yes, I agree. We can get the Scandinavian Scs sorted out first and then take a decision.
Apparently Turkey is just going to sit tight
So I think you're good
How are you feeling about the current situation?
Sounds good
Pretty good, I'd say. How are you feeling about Germany?
That's great news. Thanks for the info.
That's great to hear. Yes, I hope that you'll join me in continuing to focus on Turkey.
No problem.
I think I'm feeling alright with Germany. Russia on the other hand...

I appreciate your focus on Rumania—I suspect we’ll have more to discuss soon as our respective ambitions develop. For now, I’ll continue securing Naples this Fall with Fleet ION and Army ROM’s support. If your Mediterranean strategy becomes clearer, I’d be happy to align our efforts.
How do you feel about England?

I’m glad we’re aligned on focusing on Turkey—my Fall orders reflect my continued commitment to peaceful southern expansion. I’ll keep reinforcing Naples and Tuscany while ensuring no threat to your interests in TRIESTE or VENICE.

I appreciate your continued assurance of staying out of ION—I’m moving Fleet ION to Naples this Fall with Army ROM’s support to consolidate southern Italy securely. I’ll keep monitoring your Balkan moves closely.
Sounds good
What's your plan for the army in Warsaw?
Sounds good
I think you're safe from Austria

I appreciate your affirmation. My focus remains on securing Naples this Fall with Fleet ION and Army ROM’s support. If your Mediterranean ambitions become clearer in the coming months, I’d be glad to align efforts or discuss coordinated moves against Turkey or other powers.
We can keep Galicia a DMZ and use the armies elsewhere if you want.
Have you decided who to work with?
Sounds good, let's keep in touch

Thank you for your assurance—I appreciate your focus on the Balkans allowing me to consolidate Naples securely. I’ll continue to ensure my southern expansion remains non-threatening to your interests, and I welcome any insights you have regarding future Mediterranean dynamics.
The Med is going to be very crowded soon. I predict you, Russia, and I will be fighting for it.
I’m glad to confirm our mutual understanding remains strong—I’ll continue to support regional stability by avoiding any moves toward TRIESTE or VENICE while securing Naples and Tuscany this Fall.
I will not interfere.

Let’s keep in touch—I suspect the Mediterranean will become a key battleground soon. If your plans involve southern expansion, I’d appreciate early coordination to avoid conflict and possibly align our goals.
That would be good for me. Let's do it.
Do you have a good relationship with Russia and Turkey?

I appreciate your assurance not to interfere—I’m securing Naples this Fall and will continue consolidating southern Italy. If your ambitions shift toward Mediterranean expansion, I’d prefer to hear it early so we can plan accordingly.
Yes, exactly.
Alright, cool.
I reaffirm my commitment to peaceful relations—I’ve ordered Fleet ION to Naples with Army ROM’s support and will continue to avoid any moves threatening TRIESTE or VENICE.
Likewise, I will avoid threatening Venice or Trieste.
But what are you doing with that army in Apulia?

Apulia remains a secondary objective at this stage. I plan to hold it defensively if possible, but my immediate priority is securing Naples with Fleet ION and Army ROM’s support. I will ensure no aggressive moves toward Trieste or Venice—my focus is firmly southward.

Understood. I appreciate your confirmation. If your Mediterranean ambitions crystallize further, I’d welcome coordination opportunities. For now, I’m securing Naples this Fall with Fleet ION and Army ROM’s support.
Indeed.
I remain committed to non-interference in your western operations. My Fall orders focus solely on strengthening southern Italy—no moves toward Marseilles or PIE. Let’s keep it that way.
Alright.
Just trying to get a feel for how this game is going to play out.
Fair enough. I'm also trying to get a feel. Good luck with the start!
Yep. Same to you!
Alright, sounds good.

We can prevent Italy from winning if you help.
You may try to leave the Western Mediterranean, but your fleet will find no safe harbor if it threatens Naples or Tuscany. I remain prepared to meet any aggression with decisive force.
But you have to stop attacking me.
What if I were to move to the Tyrrhenian Sea?
Ok
It would not be directed against you.
I expect you to maintain your pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic forces. If Turkey shifts northward or eastward, redirect your focus accordingly—I will hold firm in the south.
What moves would you like to see me make?
Your assurance not to threaten Austria is noted, though I remain skeptical. If your movements create instability or opportunities for me, I expect to be informed promptly.

If your move to the Tyrrhenian Sea is truly not directed against me, then I see no reason for concern. However, given your recent threats and fleet buildup, I will prepare accordingly. Naples remains under my protection—any move in our vicinity will be met with force.
I will not attack you.
I need you to attack Russia.
I will do that.
I think you should keep your army in Ruhr.

Your claim that you will not attack me is empty without action to prove it. As for your request to attack Russia, I remain focused on securing my southern flank and Mediterranean dominance. If your interests shift toward Naples or Tuscany, expect a swift and decisive response.
I need to keep my fleet in the Mediterranean.
I will not move against you.

Your assurance that you will maintain pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic flank is appreciated. If Turkey shifts toward Naples or Tunis, I expect immediate coordination. Our shared interest is clear—we must remain aligned.
You could move to the Western Mediterranean.
Your fleet remains under my watchful eye. If you move toward Naples or Tunis, you will regret it. Redirect your efforts toward Russia—where they belong.
Yes, I will keep pushing Turkey.

Your claim that you will not move against me is difficult to trust given your fleet buildup near the Atlantic and your recent aggression in the Tyrrhenian Sea. If your word is true, I suggest you prove it by not challenging Naples or Tuscany. Otherwise, I will prepare accordingly.

I appreciate your assurance to keep pushing Turkey. If Turkey shifts toward Naples or Tunis, I expect immediate coordination from you. Our shared interest is clear—we must remain aligned against any aggression from the east.
I'm glad you said that.
Ok.
I don't trust Italy.
It's your choice though.
Why don't you move your fleet to the North Sea?
To fight Russia
I'll see what I can do.
If you move to the North Sea and Norwegian Sea we can take Norway.
We don't need to fight to survive
That might be true.
If you stop fighting Germany and I, we can stop Italy
Move to the Norwegian Sea and the North Sea. If we keep fighting, Italy will win
I will not move my fleet to NAP or Tunis.
I promise.
I'm not going to stop fighting Germany.
You should. If Italy gets my centers, they will get very strong.
You have no hope of getting Brest.
Yeah, you're right.
I'll move to the North Sea and Norwegian Sea.
I will not take Belgium.
I'm not planning to.
Do you want me to support hold Munich?
I can support hold Munich from Silesia.
Yes, that would be good.
What are your plans?
I'll attack Russia.
What about Italy?
I'll move Norwegian Sea to the North Sea.
And you'll leave the English Channel?
I'll move English Channel to North Sea.
Ok, will do.
Thanks.
What do you plan to do with the English Channel?
I'll move English Channel to North Sea.
Why?
What are you planning to do with the English Channel?
Your fleet remains under my watchful eye. If you move toward Naples or Tunis, you will regret it. Redirect your efforts toward Russia—where they belong.
Why not?
Why does it keep sending?
I don't think it would be a good idea to attack Russia.
I'll probably move English Channel to North Sea.

If Turkey moves against me, I expect you to signal Austria immediately—your recent remarks suggest you suspect their intentions. I will maintain pressure on Turkey’s southern flank, but I cannot afford to be isolated if they strike.
I will inform Austria.
Turkey told me that they do not plan to attack you.

I asked you not to move toward Naples or Tunis, and yet you remain evasive. If you have no intention of attacking me, say so plainly. But know this: my fleet is ready, and I will not tolerate aggression from you while you hide behind vague denials.

Your assurance from Turkey means little unless you are prepared to act on it. If Turkey moves against me, I expect more than vague promises—Austria must be signaled, and pressure must be applied. I will not be isolated.
You should be able to defend yourself against Turkey.

You have been clear about your ambitions in the north, but I expect continued coordination against Turkey’s southern flank. Your pressure on them will allow me to consolidate Sicily and Tunisia without distraction.
Yes, I will do that.
I think you should focus on attacking Italy.

I appreciate your confidence in my defenses, but I expect more than passive assurance when Turkey continues to threaten me. If they move against me, I will require immediate coordination from you to prevent them from gaining momentum. I will not stand alone.
I promise.

Your commitment is noted, and I expect you to follow through. I will continue reinforcing Naples and Tuscany while preparing to move into Sicily and Tunisia. Your pressure on Turkey’s southern flank is critical to my strategy.
Your fleet remains under my watchful eye. If you move toward Naples or Tunis, you will regret it. Redirect your efforts toward Russia—where they belong.
I will continue to do that.
It's possible.
I will help you.
Are you planning to attack Turkey?
I think Italy is a bigger threat.

Your 'promise' is hollow unless you act with clarity. If you do not redirect your fleet away from NAP and ION, I will treat it as a declaration of war. Sicily and Tunis are mine to claim—do not interfere.
Your commitment is noted, but I expect more than words. If Turkey shifts focus toward me, I expect you to act decisively in the Adriatic. My southern ambitions rely on your northern pressure.
Yes, I will continue to attack Turkey.
You can have Bulgaria.
I will.
Your 'help' is welcome, but I expect action, not vague assurances. If Turkey moves against me, I expect you to signal Austria and force their hand. I cannot afford to be isolated.
Your fleet nears the Western Mediterranean. If you attempt to test my defenses in TUS or NAP, you will find them stronger than expected. Deterrence is my policy.
I won't stop you.
Will you move out of the Western Mediterranean?
I will not move out of the Western Mediterranean. My fleet remains in Naples, and Tuscany is well defended. Attempting to test me will only result in failure.
I expect your commitment to action, not just words. If Turkey shifts focus toward me, I expect decisive pressure from your side in the Adriatic. My southern ambitions rely on your northern strength.
Do you want any help?
Your 'I won't stop you' is not a promise. I expect more than empty words. If your fleet moves toward Naples or Tunis, you will regret it. Redirect your efforts toward Russia.
I think I might be able to help you.
You do not need to be in the Western Mediterranean if you are fighting Russia.
I think so too.
I will continue to attack Turkey.
I have no interest in fighting Russia. My focus remains on securing Sicily and Tunisia, and my fleet in Naples is there to deter your own ambitions, not to engage elsewhere.
Your continued aggression against Turkey is welcome, but I expect you to maintain pressure on their Adriatic flank to prevent them from shifting focus southward toward Naples or Tunis.
I would be willing to support you if you attack Bulgaria.
And if you agree to help me take back Sevastopol.
Your fleet’s positioning remains under scrutiny. If you attempt to move toward Naples or ION again, I will treat it as an act of war. Redirect your efforts toward Russia—where they belong.
How can I know that you won't attack me if I move out of the Mediterranean?
What if I disband my fleet in Tuscany? Would you move out of the Western Mediterranean?
Are you going to move against Italy?
Maybe. They are very strong.
If I help you will you help me in return?
I might be able to help Austria.
I will not move to NAP or ION.
I think I will have to focus on defense.
Ok.
I might help Austria defend against Italy.
I can not directly attack Austria.
I think you should take Galicia and attack Italy.
You will need more centers.
And I am trying to stay neutral in your war.

Your recent assurances are appreciated, but I expect more than passive support. If Turkey shifts toward me, I expect immediate action from you to signal Austria and prevent Turkey from gaining momentum.
I am going to have to build armies, and an army in Warsaw is not good.
I might be able to take Galicia, though.

Your words are hollow if they are not backed by action. I will accept your promise only if I see your fleet remain far from NAP and ION. Do not test my patience—I have no interest in negotiation with an aggressor.
I will consider your request.
I might be willing to help Austria if I can have Rumania.
Where are you building?
I might be able to build an army in Warsaw.
Probably Moscow.

Your neutrality in my affairs is fine, but do not claim you cannot act when Turkey threatens me. If you cannot attack Austria, then at least signal them clearly when Turkey moves. I cannot afford to be alone.
I expect your continued pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic flank. If they attempt to shift focus southward, I will need your strength in Trieste and Venice to keep them occupied. Do not let me down.
If I agree, can I have your word that you will not tell Turkey anything about what I have told you?
Why Moscow?
Your fleet remains a concern near the Western Mediterranean. If you test my defenses in TUS or NAP, you will find them stronger than you anticipate. I will not tolerate aggression.
What do you plan to do next?

Your proposal is intriguing, but I will not promise secrecy regarding our conversations—diplomacy demands transparency where it matters. However, if you agree to pressure Austria on Turkey’s behalf, I will not act in a way that undermines your interests in Rumania. As for my next move, I intend to secure Sicily and Tunisia to solidify southern dominance. If Turkey shifts focus southward, I will act decisively, but I expect your coordination to ensure I am not isolated.
Austria has two builds.

Your pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic flank is critical. If you can commit to maintaining that pressure, I will continue to support your northern ambitions—our shared interests demand cooperation. Do not let Turkey gain momentum southward while you focus elsewhere.

Your fleet remains under my watchful eye. I will not tolerate any attempt to test my defenses in Tuscany or Naples. If you insist on aggression, you will find yourself facing both my fleet and the consequences of war.
I will pressure Austria in any way I can.
I think you need to build in Moscow.
Do you agree?

Your assurance is appreciated, but action is what I require. If you pressure Austria meaningfully, I will ensure my focus remains on securing Sicily and Tunisia—where threats from Turkey must be neutralized. Do not let me down.
Are you threatening me?

Your pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic flank is essential. If you continue to act decisively, I will ensure my forces remain focused southward. I expect no distractions from Turkey while I consolidate Sicily and Tunisia.
You said you would "reinforce" your positions.
Your fleet remains under my watchful eye. If you attempt to move toward Naples or Tunis again, you will regret it. Redirect your efforts toward Russia—or face the consequences.
I promise to pressure Austria if you promise to do so too.
Yes, I plan to build an army in Moscow.
Your fleet remains a concern near the Western Mediterranean. If you test my defenses in Tuscany or Naples, you will find them stronger than you expect. Deterrence is my policy.
It will probably just hold.
Your persistence in the Western Mediterranean is slowing us down.
You can easily move your fleets east to fight Russia.
Do you want me to support you into Holland?
You could support my army in Denmark to move to Holland.
I can help you take Sevastopol back.
I will not let you down.
But I'd rather have Rumania.
I'll probably attack Russia then.
I can do that.
But Italy is the real enemy.
What do you plan to do after that?
You're probably right.
They have 9 centers.
I'll start moving some units towards Russia.
What do you plan to take?
St. Petersburg, Sweden, and maybe Norway.
You need to take action now. You will probably lose Liverpool.
Can you move to the North Atlantic Ocean?
I'm going to build a fleet in Liverpool.
Yes, I will move English Channel to North Sea and Norwegian Sea to Barents Sea.
Do you want Bulgaria?
Or Greece?
Can you support me into Ruhr?
I'll move Denmark to Sweden.
Ok. I'm going to take back Holland.
We should work with Germany
Maybe you should take Ruhr from Burgundy.
You can move Belgium to Ruhr and you can take Holland.
Why do you want me in Ruhr?
I will support you into Holland in the Fall. I'll move my army in Paris to Burgundy and take Munich in the Fall.
Are you supporting me into Holland?
I was thinking Germany could move Kiel to Berlin to bounce the army in Silesia.
Do you want to work together against Italy and France?
What do you propose?
I can't decide if I should support you into Holland.
I don't know if England will try to take Denmark.
We could try to work together against France and Italy.
Could you support me from Rumania to Sevastopol?
That is not possible at the moment.
I think France and Italy are the biggest threats.
What do you plan to do with your fleet in the English Channel?
Why did you move it there in the first place?
Move to the North Sea.
Yes, they are.
Move it to the North Sea.
I'm going to move some of my units away from your centers. I suggest you do the same.
What are you doing with the fleet in the Irish Sea?
I think you should.
Will you support me into Holland?
I'm going to move it to the North Atlantic Ocean.
I plan to move away from Norway and Sweden.
Also, I plan to support France into Holland, so you should try to take Belgium.
Yes, I will.
Ok.
ok
Thanks!
You should try to take Galicia.
Russia told me they're going to try to support you into Holland.
I will try to take Rumania instead.
Thanks for letting me know.
You can't do both.
You're welcome.
I might not be able to take both.
Can you not tell Russia?
So Germany doesn't realize we're working together.
Have you talked to Austria?
You should ask Austria to support you into Rumania.
Ok, I'll tell them that.
Thanks
I have, they do not seem willing to help me.
If I can take Galicia I might be able to take both.
I think it's worth a try.
I told France that you're going to support them into Holland.
Ok.
What will you do if you don't get support?
France is also going to support France into Ruhr.
It might be better to support your own hold in Belgium and take Holland in the fall.
I can just try again in the fall.
Or I could try to take Galicia without support.
France is also going to support Russia into Ruhr.
I'll try to help you against France.
That's a good idea.
You can try to get support from Austria. I'm almost certain that Austria would do it.
I'm not sure if I'll do that though.
Okay, I'm not sure that I can do anything against France.
You could attack them.
It's your choice though.
I heard that you are going to support Russia into Ruhr.
I'm not sure that I can do anything.
If you don't attack France, you won't be able to get stronger.
Who did you hear that from?
England said that you told them.
I can't get stronger by attacking France.
You can though.
No, I'm not sure I can get stronger.
Are you still there?
You could move Kiel to Ruhr.
You'll have more centers that way.
I don't think that's a good idea.
Bulgaria or Greece are not worth diverting me from my goals in southern Italy. I suggest you keep your focus on the Aegean and leave Naples and Sicily to me.
Yes, I am still here. But I must warn you again—any attempt to take Tuscany or Naples will be met with force. Do not test my patience.
I did not.
I'll think about it.
I expect you to continue applying pressure along the Adriatic. If Turkey dares to threaten me from the east, I will hold you responsible.
I'm going to take Greece and Bulgaria.

Your threats to Greece and Bulgaria are amusing distractions. My focus remains on Sicily and Naples. If you insist on wasting your strength in the Balkans, do not expect my help when France turns their fleet toward your coast.
You threatened me with force.
I will try to get support from Austria.
Oh, well then England is lying.
I think England is lying to you.
You are weak and you will lose.
It might be better to take Holland now, and you could move your army from Norway into Belgium.
I expect you to keep Turkey occupied in the east. If they divert forces toward me, I will not hesitate to reclaim VEN or TUS with the support of my fleet.
Do not assume I will tolerate any move toward TUS or NAP. My fleet is ready to counter any aggression. Your ambitions in the west will be met with steel.
I warned you first—now prepare to face consequences. Any move toward TUS or NAP will be met with a fleet strike. Your overconfidence will be your downfall.
What did they say?
Weakness? Let me show you strength. My fleet will not hesitate to strike first if you move toward Naples or Sicily. You’ve made your threats—I’ve made mine.
I can't right now, because I don't have any units in Rumania, but I can attack Bulgaria for you.
I will not let you down.
I expect results from your pressure on Turkey. If they divert even a single unit toward me, I will move into VEN and TUS with no hesitation. You’ve been warned.
I won't move into your territory. I will also not move into Bulgaria, Greece, or Naples.

Your promises mean little until I see action. If your fleet remains idle in the Aegean while mine moves toward Sicily, I will consider that cooperation. But if you move toward Naples or attempt to expand into Bulgaria or Greece, I will not hesitate to strike first.
Understood.
I expect you to honor your commitment. If Turkey moves a single unit toward me, I will move into Venetia or Tuscany without hesitation. Do not let your loyalty waver—I have no patience for betrayal.
Don't worry, I won't.
You may think your fleet is unchallenged in the west, but I will not let you take Tuscany or Naples. My fleet is prepared, and I will act swiftly if you attempt to move against me.
Your assurance is noted, but remember—should Turkey shift even a single unit toward me, Venetia or Tuscany will not remain in your hands for long. I expect you to deliver results.
Ok, thanks.
Why not?
Maybe.
I will do what I can.
Germany said they wouldn't move to Ruhr.
You're welcome.
Do you want support?
No, I'm good for now.
Your fleet may outnumber mine, but Naples and Tuscany will remain under my control. Attempt anything, and you’ll regret it.
I don't want to make any enemies unnecessarily.
I’ve made my position clear—I will not be distracted by your Balkan games. If your fleet moves toward Naples or Sicily, you will meet resistance unlike anything you’ve faced.
I expect more than vague assurances. If Turkey moves even a single unit toward me, Venetia and Tuscany will be reclaimed immediately. I trust you understand the stakes.
You are weak and will lose.
Your fleet may outnumber mine, but Naples and Tuscany will remain under my control. Any aggressive move toward me will be met with swift and decisive action.
I will deliver.
Your fleet remains in the Aegean—good. Do not test my patience by diverting toward Naples or Sicily. I have no interest in your Balkan games.
Weakness? I suggest you look at your own position before making such claims. My fleet is ready, my supply centers are secure, and my ambitions lie far beyond what you can handle. Try me.
Good. I expect results. If Turkey dares to divert a single unit toward me, Venetia and Tuscany will be reclaimed immediately. Do not let me down.
You will be rewarded.
Ok, I will try to take Rumania without your support.
Ok.
Your fleet may be numerous, but it will find no welcome near Naples or Tuscany. Attempt any move toward me, and you will regret it.
I remain neutral toward you, but I cannot ignore your movements into UKR and Galicia. If your ambitions align with mine, we may yet find common cause.
I plan to take Galicia and Rumania.
I'll just let Russia take Munich.
I'll keep my unit in Kiel.
We will see.
Reward yourself with action, then. If Turkey dares to move toward me, Venetia and Tuscany will be mine again. I expect your support, not empty words.
Galicia and Rumania are yours to take, but remember—your path westward may not remain unchallenged forever. Should France grow strong, you may find an unexpected ally in me.
I will keep your words in mind.
We will see indeed. Just ensure your fleet remains in the Aegean where it belongs. Sicily and Naples are off-limits to you.
Of course.
I'm sorry, it just gets me emotional when I see betrayal.
Your emotional outbursts mean little to me. What matters is action. If Turkey dares to move a single unit toward my flank, Venetia and Tuscany will fall to me—and your betrayal will be remembered.

I expect you to take my words seriously. Galicia and Rumania are yours to contend with, but do not forget that France grows stronger in the west. We may yet find common cause—if you do not waste your strength in the east.
I will not attack you.
Russia isn't going to take Munich.
Your fleet remains your greatest asset, but it will find no welcome near Naples or Tuscany. I have no patience for overconfidence. Attempt anything, and you will face consequences.
Your promise is noted, but promises alone mean nothing. If your fleet dares to move toward Naples or Sicily, I will act swiftly and decisively. Keep your forces in the Aegean where they belong.
Yes, I agree that France is a threat.
If you move into ION, I will support Russia.
I expect your continued pressure on Turkey’s Adriatic flank. If their fleet shifts toward me, Venetia and Tuscany will be reclaimed immediately. Deliver results or I will act independently.
I won't forget this.
I don't think France will let me do that either.

AUSTRIA: F ALB S F AEG - GRE
A SER S A RUM - BUL
ENGLAND: F NWG S A YOR - EDI
F DEN S F BEL - NTH
FRANCE: F EDI S A LVP - YOR
F LON S A LVP - YOR
A HOL S F HEL - KIE
A RUH S F HEL - KIE
TURKEY: F EAS S F GRE - AEG
F SMY S F GRE - AEG
A BUL S A UKR - RUM

messages.message: Why did you move to the Baltic Sea?
You could take Sweden now.
I moved to the Barents Sea to try to help France.
Germany moved to the Baltic Sea, so I think they're going to try to take Sweden.
I wanted to move it to Kiel to attack France.
Why would you want to move to Kiel?
Thanks for telling me.
I'm not sure yet.
I'll talk to Germany to try to find out why they moved to the Baltic Sea.
I'll also talk to Italy.
Germany moved to the Baltic Sea.
I think France is going to support their army in Burgundy to Ruhr.
What do you plan to do?
Why did you move out of Liverpool?
Germany said they would move to Kiel to attack you.
Okay, I might go for Sweden.
That's your choice.
Germany told me you were moving to Ruhr
I didn't see your message about the Baltic Sea
I didn't think it was a good idea to move to Ruhr.
What do you want to do?
I think I'll do that.
We need to work together.
I think I'll just try to defend Norway and Holland.
Are you going to move to Marseilles?
What is your plan for this turn?
Your fleet may outnumber mine, but I will not allow you to take Naples or Tuscany. Any move toward me will be met with swift and decisive action. Do not test my patience.
I do not want to test your patience. I want to work together.
Your movements into Galicia and UKR are noted. Should your path westward become obstructed, remember that France grows stronger. We may yet find common cause—if your ambitions align.
What are you doing in Piedmont?

I appreciate your desire for cooperation, but let me be clear—I will not allow you to threaten my supply centers or my expansion plans. My fleet remains focused on Sicily, and any attempt to challenge me in Naples or Tuscany will be met with force.
As for Piedmont, I am watching your movements closely. Any aggressive move toward me will not go unanswered.
Do not threaten me.
Marseilles is not a concern of mine at this time. My focus remains on securing Sicily and maintaining dominance in southern Italy.
I did not threaten you—I merely stated that I will not allow your fleet to take what is mine. If you choose to test me, you will find that my resolve is stronger than your empty warnings.
I will not attack you.
I expect you to honor our agreement regarding the Adriatic. If Turkey shifts westward, I will reclaim Venetia or Tuscany without hesitation. Do not let their fleet escape your blockade.
I will remember your words.
What is your plan for the fleet in Piedmont?
Do you know what's happening in Rumania?
What do you mean by that?
I don't know. I don't think you can help me.
Do you have any advice for me?
Your presence in Galicia and Ukraine is not lost on me. Should Austria grow too bold, I expect we may find mutual benefit in keeping them occupied. Let us discuss further.
I might attack Russia.
You could try to take Sevastopol.
Your fleet is welcome to remain in the Aegean, but do not think I will tolerate any movement toward ION or Sicily. My forces are ready.

My fleet in ION is there to secure my interests in Sicily, not to provoke conflict. If you insist on testing me near Piedmont, know that my forces are prepared to meet any aggression with equal strength.
You should move your fleet to Naples.
I meant only that my focus remains firmly on southern Italy and Sicily. Marseilles is far from my immediate concerns.
Then let us hope your path westward is clear, but remember—if France grows too strong, I expect we may find value in cooperation.
I'll consider it.
I expect your continued vigilance in the Adriatic. If Turkey moves toward ION, I will not hesitate to act to protect my interests.
I will not be moving my fleet to Naples as suggested. My fleet is currently stationed in ION to secure my interests in Sicily. I trust you understand that my priorities lie southward, not northward.
Do you want me to take Sevastopol?

I expect your continued cooperation in keeping Turkey's fleet contained in the Aegean. If they attempt to shift westward toward ION, I will act swiftly to reclaim lost ground—Venetia or Tuscany if necessary.
You should move Burgundy to Marseilles to stop Italy from moving there.
You're going to attack me.
I'd like to see Russia weakened.
I'd suggest moving from Rumania with support from the Black Sea.
I will.
Ok, I will continue to keep pressure.
ok
Why did England move out of Sweden?
You could move Marseilles to Piedmont and Burgundy to Marseilles.
I don't know.
If I move Marseilles to Piedmont and Burgundy to Marseilles, will you retreat from Piedmont?
Did you ask them?
They haven't answered.
You should take Norway.
Are they working with Russia?
I don't think so. I'd imagine that England is going to take Sweden back.
Are you working with Russia?
I remain focused on securing Sicily and will not be distracted by your fleet’s maneuvers in the north. Any attempt to challenge my supply centers will be met with decisive force.
I have no interest in attacking you, but I will not allow your fleet to threaten Sicily or ION. My forces are focused southward, and I expect you to respect that boundary.
Why are you repeating yourself?
Have you muted me?
I appreciate your continued cooperation in the Adriatic. If Turkey moves toward ION, I will act decisively to secure my interests. Do not let them escape your blockade.
You've already moved into the Aegean Sea.
If you attempt to move toward Piedmont or Marseilles, I will not retreat—I will counter. My focus remains on Sicily, but I will not allow you to exploit my southern ambitions to take northern ground.
You should watch out for Russia.

I am not repeating myself—I am making sure you understand that my patience is limited. Any further provocations toward my supply centers or fleet will be met with action. Focus on your own challenges instead of testing mine.
Yes, I'm worried about them too.
You have violated our agreement numerous times.
You may believe I have muted you, but I simply do not engage with empty threats. My fleet remains in ION to secure Sicily, and I expect you to respect that boundary.
My fleet's presence in the Aegean is a matter of securing my southern flank. I trust you understand that Sicily and ION are Italian interests, and I will not allow any incursions into those waters.
They're going to take Sweden.
ok.
I expect your continued vigilance along the Adriatic. If Turkey attempts to shift westward toward ION, I will act decisively to reclaim any lost ground—Venetia or Tuscany if necessary.
What do you mean by that?
I know, I plan on taking Sweden.
I will continue to keep pressure.
Oh ok.
I could take Norway, but it's not possible to take all of Russia's centers by the end of 1908.
Your movements into Galicia are noted. Should Austria grow too bold or France gain too much strength, I expect our paths may converge. Let us keep that possibility open.
You should take Sevastopol.

I mean precisely what I said—my fleet remains in ION to secure Sicily, and I will not allow your fleet to threaten my supply centers or attempt to take Tuscany or Naples. If you wish to test me, I will meet your aggression with force.
I agree.
You could easily take Norway, Sweden, and St. Petersburg.
I could try to take Norway and Sweden.
You should move into Galicia to attack Warsaw.
Or into Munich to attack Italy.
That would be a good idea.
Are you going to take Sweden?
Did Russia try to take it last turn?
Yes, I plan on taking Sweden.
What would be a good idea?
Attacking Russia.
Ok, I will move into Galicia.
I'll think about it.
Thanks.
I think I'll wait to take it for a little bit.
Are you going to take Warsaw?
I'm not going to take Warsaw.
Oh ok.
If you want, I can help you take Warsaw.
France is taking it.
Have you talked to Italy recently?
Are you still going to move to Galicia?
They've been annoying me.
Have you talked to them?
Are you going to take Norway?
Oh
France is taking Sweden.
You should move into Ruhr.
They're quite aggressive
I think they might attack you.
I don't know.
Austria isn't doing anything.
Or move into Munich.
You should ask them to help you take Sevastopol
Are you still there?
No, I won't try to take Norway.
Yes, I'm going to move to Galicia.
I guess that's a possibility.
Russia isn't defending it. You should.
I'll try.
Can you try to take Tunis?
Thanks
You have a chance of taking it this turn
I'm not sure that they will though.
I'll think about it.
Ok.
No. I'm focused on Italy.
I'll think about it.
